hawkish_phrases: Further tightening in monetary policy would be required if there were evidence of more persistent inflationary pressures
Monetary policy will need to be sufficiently restrictive for sufficiently long
risks around the modal inflation projection were skewed to the upside
Second-round effects in domestic prices and wages were expected to take longer to unwind than they had done to emerge
The labour market had nevertheless remained relatively tight
dovish_phrases: GDP is expected to grow by 0.1% in Q4, also weaker than projected previously
employment growth is likely to have softened over the second half of 2023
There had been increasing signs of some impact of tighter monetary policy on momentum in the real economy
Twelve-month CPI inflation fell to 6.7% both in September and 2023 Q3, below expectations in the August Report
For one member, the risks of overtightening policy had continued to build
summary: The MPC voted 6-3 to hold Bank Rate at 5.25% with three members preferring a 25bp hike to 5.5%, maintaining a clearly restrictive stance with explicit guidance that policy needs to remain restrictive for an extended period and that further tightening would be required if inflationary pressures prove more persistent.
